title: Data Scientist- AI Engineer
company_focus:
Enterprise automation software for mission-critical business
processes and ERP orchestration
description:
OUR MISSION

At Redwood, we empower our customers with lights-out
automation for their mission-critical business processes.

ABOUT US

Redwood Software is the leader in full stack automation
fabric solutions for mission-critical business processes.
With the first SaaS-based composable automation platform
specifically built for ERP, we believe in the transformative
power of automation. Our unparalleled solutions empower you
to orchestrate, manage and monitor your workflows across any
application, service or server — in the cloud or on premises
— with confidence and control.

Redwood’s global team of automation experts and customer
success engineers provide solutions and world-class support
designed to give you the freedom and time to imagine and
define your future. Get out of the weeds and see the forest,
with Redwood Software.

CORE VALUES

One Team. One Redwood

Make Your Own Weather

Obsess over Customer Success

Work the Problem

Be Curious

Own the Outcome

Respect Each Other



YOUR IMPACT

As the Data Scientist – AI Engineer, you will transform
revenue data into AI-powered intelligence that drives
smarter, faster, and more predictive decision-making across
the business. You will lead the application of advanced
analytics, machine learning, and generative AI to uncover
patterns in sales performance, customer behavior, and
revenue dynamics—turning complex data into clear strategic
direction. By building scalable predictive models and
embedding AI into revenue workflows, you will help forecast
growth, reduce churn, optimize sales strategies, and
identify high-value opportunities. Your work will directly
influence executive decision-making, accelerate insight
generation, and position AI as a core driver of measurable
business impact across the revenue organization.

Data Analysis, AI-Driven Insights & Reporting:

●Conduct in-depth analysis of sales cycles, conversion
rates, customer churn, and other revenue-driving activities
using advanced AI-powered analytics tools and large-scale
data processing techniques to identify trends,
opportunities, and areas for improvement.

●Leverage generative AI, machine learning platforms, and
automated analytics solutions to accelerate insight
generation and improve analytical depth and speed.

●Translate complex, AI-derived data findings into clear,
concise, and compelling narratives for various stakeholders,
including executive leadership.

●Perform ad-hoc, AI-assisted data analysis to support
strategic initiatives and answer critical business
questions, utilizing automation to increase responsiveness
and analytical scalability.

Data Science & Predictive Modeling:

●Apply advanced statistical methods, machine learning, and
AI techniques (including supervised, unsupervised, and deep
learning approaches where appropriate) to forecast revenue,
predict customer behavior (e.g., churn risk, upsell
potential), and optimize sales strategies.

● Design, develop, and deploy AI-driven predictive models to
segment customers, identify ideal customer profiles (ICPs),
enhance lead scoring, and automate decision-making
processes.

● Utilize AI development frameworks and MLOps best practices
to build scalable, production-ready models and continuously
improve model performance.

●Collaborate with stakeholders to define business problems
and translate them into AI-powered data science solutions,
proactively identifying opportunities where AI can create
measurable business impact.

●Continuously evaluate, monitor, and refine models for
accuracy, fairness, and impact, leveraging automated
retraining, experimentation platforms, and model performance
tracking.

●Champion the responsible and effective use of AI across
revenue analytics, ensuring transparency, governance, and
alignment with business objectives.



YOUR EXPERIENCE

- Bachelor's degree in Business Analytics, Data Science,
  Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related
  quantitative field. Master's degree preferred.
- 3+ years of experience in a data science role, preferably
  within a Revenue Operations or Sales Operations function
  at a B2B software company.
- Proven experience with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and
  marketing automation platforms.
- Strong experience with SQL for data extraction,
  manipulation, and analysis.
- Experience with statistical analysis and predictive modeling
  using Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn)
- Experience using AI-assisted analytics tools (LLM copilots,
  automated feature engineering, AutoML, etc.)
- Ability to design AI workflows that accelerate decision-
  making with tools like Claude Code, ChatGPT and Gemini

Additional Skills:

- Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with a
  keen eye for detail.
- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-
  paced environment.
- Strong communication and presentation skills, with the
  ability to explain complex data concepts to non-technical
  audiences.
- Proactive, self-starter with a strong sense of ownership and
  accountability.
- Understanding of sales processes, SaaS business models, and
  key revenue metrics.
- Comfort integrating generative AI (LLMs) into workflows for
  insight generation, summarization, or automation

If you like growth and working with happy, enthusiastic
over-achievers, you'll enjoy your career with us!

THE LEGAL BIT Redwood is an equal opportunity employer.
Redwood prohibits unlawful discrimination based on race,
colour, religion, sex, gender identity, marital or veteran
status, age, national origin, ancestry, citizenship,
physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic
information or characteristics (or those of a family
member), sexual orientation, pregnancy or any other
consideration made unlawful by regional or local laws. We
also prohibit discrimination based on a perception that
anyone has any of those characteristics or is associated
with a person who has or is perceived as having any of those
characteristics. All such discrimination is unlawful and
will have a zero tolerance policy applied to it.


Redwood will comply with all local data protection laws,
including GDPR when it comes to the handling and processing
of personal data. Should you wish for us to remove your
personal data from our recruitment database, please email us
directly at Recruitment@Redwood.com
responsibilities: Conduct in-depth analysis of sales cycles, conversion rates, customer churn using AI-powered analytics and large-scale data processing
Apply advanced statistical methods, machine learning, and AI techniques to forecast revenue and predict customer behavior
Design, develop, and deploy AI-driven predictive models for customer segmentation, lead scoring, and automated decision-making
Build scalable production-ready models using AI development frameworks and MLOps best practices
Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business problems into AI-powered data science solutions
Translate complex AI-derived data findings into clear narratives for executive leadership
Continuously evaluate, monitor, and refine models for accuracy, fairness, and impact using automated retraining and experimentation platforms
Champion responsible and effective use of AI across revenue analytics with transparency and governance
use_cases: Customer churn prediction and retention optimization
Customer segmentation and ideal customer profile identification
Lead scoring enhancement and sales strategy optimization
Automated decision-making processes for revenue operations
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building, deploying, and maintaining AI-
driven predictive models using ML techniques and generative
AI. The position requires designing AI workflows, applying
ML/AI techniques for forecasting and predictions, and
deploying production-ready models with MLOps practices.
While focused on revenue analytics, it's hands-on work
building and implementing AI/ML systems.
